test.name: FindMyPast Irish birth search via the site-wide fallback template
test.description: FindMyPast is the strongest fit for Irish records, and FamilySearch curates no external links for Ireland in this window (zero-results fixture) — so the skill must fall back to the site-wide FindMyPast URL template rather than a curated collection URL. Patrick Flynn's Irish birth (~1845) is the project objective's origin question.

input.user_message: Search FindMyPast for Patrick Flynn's birth in Ireland, about 1845.

judge_context: external_links_search returns totalForPlace 0 — the skill should recognize FamilySearch curates nothing for this place and fall back to the site-wide FindMyPast template (findmypast.com/search/results) rather than inventing a curated collection URL
Generated URL should carry FindMyPast's parameter names (firstname, lastname, yearofbirth, keywordsplace or equivalent) filled from the subject: Patrick Flynn, birth about 1845, Ireland
Should provide capture-workflow instructions (click, save results as PDF, upload back) and note a login may be required since FindMyPast is a paid site
A research-log entry must be written in the same turn the URL is generated: site findmypast, outcome partial, capture_received false
